title: Child Guidance * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues - 211info
text: - Radius - 5 Miles 10 Miles 25 Miles 50 Miles 100 Miles Search by Provider Name > New Search > Foster Families > Child Guidance * Adoption/Foster Care/Kinship Care Issues 4 Matching Service Providers Print & Share All Results X Print & Share Print PDF 211INFO 211INFO RESOURCE PARENT SUPPORT Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Offers support, resource referral, and additional after-hours support for foster parents. Eligibility: Any foster parent with an ODHS Child Welfare case or in the Oregon Foster Care System Hours: 24 hours per day / 7 days per week Email 211 Visit Website More Details FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON FAMILY SOLUTIONS ASHLAND PROCTOR FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individualized community-based treatment for children and youth with severe emotional and behavioral issues. Children and resource (foster) parents are matched, one to two children per home. Children receive individual, group, and family therapy. Skills training, mentoring, case management, and other individualized services offered. Respite care available to foster parents. This is an alternative to BRS residential care, allowing the child to remain in a home environment and community setting. 1836 Fremont Street Ashland, OR 97520 Eligibility: Ages 4-18 who have behavioral issues that require them to be placed in a more specialized foster home with extra support. Must be involved with ODHS Child Welfare. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm Email (541) 916-4777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON FAMILY SOLUTIONS GRANTS PASS PROCTOR FOSTER CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides individualized community-based treatment for children and youth with severe emotional and behavioral issues. Children and resource (foster) parents are matched, one to two children per home. Children receive individual, group, and family therapy. Skills training, mentoring, case management, and other individualized services offered. Respite care available to foster parents. This is an alternative to BRS residential care, allowing the child to remain in a home environment and community setting. 322 NW F Street Grants Pass, OR 97526 Eligibility: Ages 4-18 who have behavioral issues that require them to be placed in a more specialized foster home with extra support. Must be involved with ODHS Child Welfare. Hours: Monday-Friday 8am-5pm (541) 916-4777 Get Directions Visit Website More Details KINSHIP HOUSE OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CARE Print & Share X Print & Share Print PDF Provides outpatient mental health therapy and advocacy to children and their foster or adoptive families. Specialized child welfare assessments for parent-child interaction, siblings, and individual needs provided statewide. Offers professional training and community education, including workshops, support groups, and appropriate medical referrals. 1823 NE 8th Avenue Portland, OR 97212 Eligibility: Age 18 and younger who are in the foster care system or involved in the adoption process Hours: Monday-Friday 9am-5pm Email (503) 460-2796 106 Get Directions Visit Website More Details FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON PROCTOR FOSTER CARE FAMILY SOLUTIONS SOUTHERN OREGON PROCTOR FOSTER CARE KINSHIP HOUSE OUTPATIENT MENTAL HEALTH CARE
